title: storage-v3 #6: snapshot cache port (ref-set key)
body:
# Goal

Port the snapshot cache
(`.jj/jjforge-cache.json`,
`crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs::snapshot` at lines 1026–1059
ish) from "keyed on the `issues` bookmark tip sha" to "keyed
on the set of `refs/jjf/issues/*` and `refs/jjf/memories/*`
ref shas."

End state: bulk reads (`Storage::list_records`,
`Storage::list_ready`, `Storage::list_memories`,
`Storage::snapshot`) hit a warm cache when the ref set is
unchanged and rebuild only when the ref set changes. The cache
is invalidated correctly on every v3 mutation.

# Why now

Ticket 6 of 8 in the v3 storage epic (`bd98097`). Depends on
tickets 1, 2, 3, 4 — needs the v3 write/read path to exist and
to have moved data onto the new refs. Without the cache port,
bulk reads on a v3 repo cost N git calls every time (~1–2s for
968 issues); with the cache, ~10ms warm.

# Approach

Today's cache key is the `issues` bookmark tip sha. Any
mutation moves the bookmark, so any mutation invalidates the
cache. Simple, correct.

In v3, mutations move per-issue refs, not a shared bookmark.
The cache key needs to reflect the FULL SET of issue refs.

**Cache key shape.**

Option 1: SHA of the concatenation of all `refs/jjf/issues/*`
+ `refs/jjf/memories/*` sha pairs in sorted order. Cheap to
compute (`git for-each-ref --sort=refname --format='%(refname)
%(objectname)'` → SHA-256). One-shot invalidation check.

Option 2: A per-ref shadow in the cache file — a map from
ref name to (sha-at-cache-build, record). On read, enumerate
current refs and compare each sha; if any differs, mark only
THAT issue stale and re-read it. Finer-grained invalidation,
slightly more complex.

**Recommendation: Option 1 first.** It matches the v2 cache's
all-or-nothing invalidation semantics. Option 2 is a follow-up
optimization if the rebuild cost matters (it probably won't —
rebuilds are ~10ms warm cache + ~1–2s cold for 968 issues, and
the cold rebuild only happens after a write).

**Cache structure on disk.** Same `.jj/jjforge-cache.json`
file path. Schema mostly the same — full snapshot of all
issues + memories, keyed by id/key. Just the top-level cache
key field changes from bookmark-tip-sha to ref-set-sha.

**Invalidation point.** Every v3 write path (from ticket 1)
calls `cache.invalidate()` after a successful `git update-ref`.
Same shape as today's "after `try_commit_dance` succeeds,
invalidate the memo."

# Scope

In scope:
- Change the cache key computation to enumerate `refs/jjf/issues/*`
  and `refs/jjf/memories/*` and SHA-256 the sorted ref+sha
  pairs.
- Wire cache invalidation into the v3 write path.
- Keep the cache file path (`.jj/jjforge-cache.json`)
  unchanged — operators won't notice.
- Keep the v2 cache path operational while a repo is still
  v2-shape (i.e. cache key falls back to bookmark tip when
  v3 refs absent). Once a repo is v3, the cache key is
  ref-set-derived.

Out of scope:
- Per-ref granular cache (Option 2). File as a follow-up if
  the all-or-nothing rebuild cost is measurable.
- Cache file format change. Keep the JSON shape; just swap the
  key field.
- Disk-format versioning of the cache. A simple "if cache
  format unknown, ignore and rebuild" is fine.

# Acceptance

- [ ] Bulk read on a v3 repo with a warm cache returns in
      ~10ms (qualitative; just verify the cache is being
      hit by checking `git for-each-ref` is not invoked
      again).
- [ ] Bulk read on a v3 repo with a cold cache rebuilds the
      cache and writes it to disk.
- [ ] Any v3 mutation invalidates the cache (next read
      rebuilds it).
- [ ] On a v2-shape repo, the cache continues to use the
      bookmark-tip-sha key (no regression).
- [ ] The existing `cache_hit_avoids_rebuild_n_issues`
      integration test passes against a v3 repo.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` green.

# Pointers

- Design doc: `docs/storage-out-of-tree.md` (search for
  "Snapshot cache").
- Code to port: `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs::snapshot`
  (around line 1026–1059), and the cache file write path.
- Existing test that asserts cache behavior:
  `cache_hit_avoids_rebuild_n_issues` (integration test).

# Out-of-scope reminder

Do NOT bypass the cache for bulk reads on v3 — that would
regress the performance benefit. Do NOT change the cache file
path; ops would lose their warm cache silently.
